description: EPLAN Electric P8 is a professional electrical CAD/CAE software used for planning, documenting, and managing electrical engineering projects. This project tree represents the standard report/document structure generated within an EPLAN P8 project.

forms.description: Graphical representation of cables used in the project, showing routing, assignment, and connection details.
forms.used_for: Documenting cable runs between devices, showing cable type, cross-section, length, and shield information.
forms.when_to_use: Use when your project involves routed cables between control panels, machines, or field devices.
forms.example: A cable from a main distribution panel to a motor starter cabinet — shows cable ID (W1), type (NYCWY 3x1.5mm²), from/to terminals, and length (15m).

individual_vs_total.Individual: One page per cable — detailed view of each cable with its conductors.
individual_vs_total.Total: All cables listed in a summary table across the entire project.

forms.description: A tabular overview listing all cables in the project with key attributes such as cable designation, type, length, and connected devices.
forms.used_for: Procurement planning, cable scheduling, and quick reference for installation teams.
forms.when_to_use: Use during the procurement or installation phase to get a consolidated list of all cables.
forms.example: A table listing W1 (15m, NYCWY 3x1.5), W2 (8m, H05VV-F 2x1.0), W3 (22m, ÖLFLEX 5G2.5) with source and destination terminals.

individual_vs_total.Individual: Overview per cable including conductor details.
individual_vs_total.Total: Summary across all cables in the project.

forms.description: A tabular list of all electrical connections (wires/conductors) in the project, including source terminal, target terminal, wire color, cross-section, and potential.
forms.used_for: Used by wiring technicians during panel assembly; serves as the definitive wiring reference.
forms.when_to_use: Use during cabinet wiring and commissioning. Essential for point-to-point wiring without needing to read schematics.
forms.example: Row: From=X1:1, To=K1:A1, Wire=BK, 1.5mm², Potential=L1 — means terminal X1 pin 1 connects to contactor K1 coil terminal A1 using a 1.5mm² black wire at potential L1.

subform_notes.Electrical: Lists all signal and power connections defined in the electrical schematics.

forms.description: A structured list of all device tags (DT) used in the project, showing technical data, article numbers, descriptions, and assigned functions.
forms.used_for: Device management, BOM (Bill of Materials) generation, and cross-referencing components to their datasheet/part numbers.
forms.when_to_use: Use during engineering design review, procurement, and asset management phases.
forms.example: Tag: =GB1+ET1-K1, Description: Main contactor, Article: 3RT2026-1AP00 (Siemens), Qty: 1, Function: Motor starter.

individual_vs_total.Individual: One report per location or functional group.
individual_vs_total.Total: Complete device list across the entire project.

forms.fluid_note: Fluid variants cover pneumatic/hydraulic components tagged in P&ID or fluid power diagrams.

forms.description: Piping and Instrumentation Diagram (P&ID) overview page showing process flow, instrumentation, and control elements according to ISA/IEC standards.
forms.used_for: Process engineering documentation showing relationship between process equipment, piping, and instrumentation.
forms.when_to_use: Use in projects involving process plants, HVAC systems, water treatment, or any process with fluid flow and instrumentation.
forms.example: A P&ID sheet showing a pump (P-101), flow transmitter (FT-101), control valve (FCV-101) with associated piping lines and signal connections to a DCS.

forms.description: Automatically generated PLC connection diagrams showing I/O card assignments, terminal connections, and signal names linked to PLC addresses.
forms.used_for: Documenting PLC hardware configuration and wiring. Links field instruments/actuators to specific PLC I/O channels.
forms.when_to_use: Use in any automation project where a PLC is present. Generates wiring diagrams from PLC I/O planning data.
forms.example: CPU: Siemens S7-1500, Card: DI 32x24VDC, Channel I0.0 = 'Emergency Stop S1', Terminal X10:1 → PLC card slot 2, pin 1.

subform_notes.SPS Connection: SPS is the German abbreviation for PLC (Speicherprogrammierbare Steuerung). Shows terminal-to-PLC-pin wiring.

forms.description: Early-stage pipe class planning sheets showing pipe specifications, pressure ratings, material classes, and applicable standards before detailed design.
forms.used_for: Pre-engineering and FEED (Front End Engineering Design) phases for process projects.
forms.when_to_use: Use at project kickoff to define piping specifications before detailed P&ID or isometric work begins.
forms.example: Pipe class A1B: Carbon steel, PN40, temp range -20°C to +300°C, applicable to steam lines in utility system.

individual_vs_total.Diagram: Schematic representation of pipe class attributes.
individual_vs_total.Overview: Tabular summary of all defined pipe classes.

forms.description: High-level planning objects representing functional units, control loops, and device groups before detailed engineering diagrams are created.
forms.used_for: Top-down project planning, scope definition, and handover between process and electrical/instrumentation engineering.
forms.when_to_use: Use in early project phases to plan PCT (process control technology) loops and define functional requirements.
forms.example: PCT Loop: TIC-101 (Temperature Indication & Control) — includes TT-101 (temperature transmitter), TCV-101 (control valve), linked to DCS controller.

subform_notes.Cable planning objects: Planned cables not yet fully engineered — placeholder for cable scheduling.
subform_notes.PCT loop overview: Summary of all instrumentation and control loops defined in pre-planning.
subform_notes.Planning object -Device list: List of all devices assigned to planning objects/functional units.

forms.description: Loop diagrams for instrumentation and control circuits showing signal flow from field device through junction box, marshalling, and into the control system.
forms.used_for: Instrumentation engineering, loop checking during commissioning, and as-built documentation.
forms.when_to_use: Use for all PCT loops in process automation projects. Mandatory for instrumentation FAT/SAT testing.
forms.example: Loop TIC-101: Field device TT-101 (4-20mA, HART) → JB-05 terminal 3/4 → Marshalling cabinet MC-01 → AI card slot 3 ch.7 → DCS tag TIC101_PV.

forms.description: Documentation of process substances/fluids (media) used in the plant, including properties like temperature, pressure, phase, and hazard classification.
forms.used_for: Process safety documentation, material selection, and compliance with ATEX/SIL requirements.
forms.when_to_use: Use in chemical, oil & gas, or any process project where fluid properties influence engineering decisions.
forms.example: Substance: Natural Gas, Phase: Gas, Temp: -10 to +50°C, Pressure: 10 bar, ATEX Zone: 1, Hazard class: II B T3.

individual_vs_total.Diagram: Process diagram annotated with substance information.
individual_vs_total.Overview: Table of all substances with their properties.

forms.description: Overview of all structure identifiers used in the project — including higher-level assignment (=), installation location (+), and function definition (-) according to IEC 81346.
forms.used_for: Project structure verification, tag naming convention review, and navigation reference.
forms.when_to_use: Use during project setup review and for clients/auditors who need to understand the tag naming logic.
forms.example: =GB1 (Functional assignment: Drive system), +ET1 (Installation location: Control cabinet 1), -K1 (Device: Contactor 1) → Full tag: =GB1+ET1-K1.

forms.description: Consolidated Bill of Materials (BOM) summing identical parts across the entire project or per functional group, with quantities and article numbers.
forms.used_for: Procurement, warehouse management, and cost estimation.
forms.when_to_use: Use at the end of engineering to generate the final parts list for purchasing department.
forms.example: Article: 3RT2026-1AP00, Description: Siemens Contactor 11kW, Qty: 5, Unit price: €45.00, Total: €225.00.

individual_vs_total.Individual: BOM grouped by location or page set.
individual_vs_total.Total: Complete project-wide BOM with summarized quantities.

forms.description: Automatically generated document index listing all schematic pages with page numbers, descriptions, and page types.
forms.used_for: Document navigation and formal document control submission.
forms.when_to_use: Always include in final documentation packages delivered to clients or stored in document management systems.
forms.example: Page 1: Cover sheet | Page 2–15: Circuit diagrams =GB1 | Page 16–18: Terminal diagrams | Page 19: Parts list.

subform_notes.Total: Single comprehensive TOC for the entire project.

forms.description: Graphical or tabular overview of terminal strips showing terminal arrangement, designations, cross-connections, bridge groups, and connected conductors.
forms.used_for: Cabinet assembly, wiring inspection, and terminal labeling.
forms.when_to_use: Mandatory for all projects with terminal blocks. Used by panel builders for physical terminal arrangement.
forms.example: Strip X1: Terminal 1 (PE, green-yellow), Terminal 2 (L1, brown, 2.5mm²) → Motor M1 U1, Terminal 3 (L2, black) → Motor M1 V1.

individual_vs_total.Individual: One page per terminal strip with full graphical layout.
individual_vs_total.Total: Summary table of all terminal strips and their properties.

forms.description: Detailed wiring diagram of individual terminals showing internal/external connections, cable references, and signal flow direction (incoming/outgoing).
forms.used_for: Precise wiring instructions for technicians; shows exactly what connects to each terminal pin.
forms.when_to_use: Use for complex terminal strips with many external cable connections, especially in marshalling or junction boxes.
forms.example: Terminal X1:5: Internal wire from -K3:14 (NC contact), External cable W5 conductor 3 (BU, 0.75mm²) to field solenoid -Y1.

individual_vs_total.Total: All terminal diagrams in one report.

forms.description: The front matter of the documentation package including project title block, material compliance declarations, and safety/handling instructions.
forms.used_for: Formal document identification, regulatory compliance, and safety communication.
forms.when_to_use: Always required for any documentation deliverable, especially for customer handover or regulatory submission.
forms.example: Cover sheet: Project Name, Customer, Revision history, Drawn by, Approved by, Date. Material regulations: RoHS/REACH compliance. Safety instructions: Lockout-Tagout procedures.

subform_notes.Cover sheet: Standard title block with project metadata and revision history.
subform_notes.Material regulations: Declaration of compliance with RoHS, REACH, or customer-specific material restrictions.
subform_notes.Safety instructions: General electrical safety notes, lockout/tagout requirements, and applicable standards (IEC 60364, VDE).

eplan_p8_general_notes.form_file_location: Forms are stored as *.f?? files in the EPLAN master data directory: %APPDATA%\EPLAN\Electric P8\{version}\Forms\
eplan_p8_general_notes.report_generation: Reports are generated via: Utilities > Reports > Generate (or Edit Reports dialog)
eplan_p8_general_notes.form_customization: Each form can be customized in the Form Editor (graphical layout tool built into EPLAN P8)
key_standards: IEC 61082 — Preparation of documents used in electrotechnology
